$schema_doc: Eval test case template consumed by agent-audit. The skill_name field names the target skill. The evals array holds 3-5 test cases generated by the LLM in Step 2. Each test case starts with id, prompt, expected_output. After Step 3, actual_output is filled in. After Step 4 (first run only), assertions is populated. After Step 5, each assertion gains passed and evidence. Verdict is computed by aggregating assertion results.

$assertions_doc.rule: Write 3-5 assertions per test case in Step 4, AFTER actual_output is populated. Do NOT write assertions before actual_output exists.
$assertions_doc.must_be: Specific and verifiable — name an observable fact: a file path, a count, a quoted string, a section heading, a boolean outcome.
$assertions_doc.must_not_be: Vague or subjective — never write 'output is good', 'response is helpful', or 'quality is high'.
$assertions_doc.human_review_flag: Set human_review: true only when the assertion cannot be decided by code or LLM — e.g. visual layout balance, tonal register, subjective appropriateness. At most 1-2 per eval.
$assertions_doc.evidence_rule: In Step 5, evidence must be a direct quote from actual_output (for PASS) or the specific gap that makes it false (for FAIL). Benefit of the doubt always fails.

good: Top-fixes list contains at least 3 items
Skill emits Step 1/5 through Step 5/5 headers in order
No finding in the review uses the phrase 'looks good'

evals.prompt:
Please review this design. Here is the PRD: This is a food delivery app home screen. Goal: users can browse restaurants and start an order within 2 taps. Audience: mobile users on iOS. Success metric: 80% of users reach the restaurant menu page within 30 seconds of opening the app.

Screenshot: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/afgprogrammer/Flutter-food-deliveryapp-ui/master/assets/screenshot/one.png
evals.expected_output: A severity-grouped markdown review (Blocker / Major / Minor / Nit sections) with at least one finding per section where applicable, each finding naming the broken state and user consequence. Followed by a top_fixes ordered list of 3-5 items ranked by user impact. Step 1/5 through Step 5/5 headers emitted in order.
evals.actual_output: Emitted Step 1/5 through Step 5/5. Blocker: 3 findings (no loading state; no location-denied fallback; no offline indicator). Major: 5 findings. Minor: 3 findings. Nit: 2 findings. Top Fixes: 5 items ranked by user impact.

assertions.text: Output contains 'Step 1/5' through 'Step 5/5' headers in order
Output includes a section headed 'Blocker' with at least one finding
Output includes a 'Top Fix' or 'Top Fixes' section with at least 3 items
Each finding in the Blocker section names a broken state and a user consequence in one sentence
Output does not contain the phrase 'looks good'

evals.prompt:
audit this UI — I need a design review. Context from the PRD: This is a food delivery restaurant listing screen. Users tap on a restaurant card to view its menu. Key user goal: find and select a restaurant in under 10 seconds. Target audience: hungry users on a time constraint, mobile (Android). Success metric: task completion rate of 85%.

Please check the screenshot here: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/afgprogrammer/Flutter-food-delivery-app-ui/master/assets/screenshot/two.png
evals.expected_output: Severity-grouped review with findings referencing specific UI elements visible in the screenshot (e.g., tap targets, text contrast, empty states). Top-fixes section with 3-5 ranked items. Step headers Step 1/5 through Step 5/5 present. No finding uses the phrase 'looks good'.
evals.actual_output: Emitted Step 1/5 through Step 5/5. Scope: in-scope. Blocker: 4 findings (card entity mismatch; single card per viewport; contrast failure; no loading state). Major: 8 findings including no search, no offline, chip truncation. Minor: 5 findings. Nit: 3 findings. Top Fixes: 5 items.

assertions.text: Output contains 'Step 1/5' through 'Step 5/5' headers in order
Output includes a section headed 'Blocker' or 'BLOCKER' with at least one finding
Output includes a finding about contrast or tap target size referencing a specific UI element
Output includes a Top Fixes section with 3-5 ranked items
Output does not contain the phrase 'looks good'

evals.prompt:
critique this flow — I want you to do a design review on the third screen of our food delivery app. PRD context: This is the order confirmation / checkout summary screen. The user has selected items and is reviewing their order before placing it. User goal: confirm order details and place order with confidence. Audience: first-time users on iOS, unfamiliar with the app. Success metric: order placement without support contact.

Image: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/afgprogrammer/Flutter-food-delivery-app-ui/master/assets/screenshot/three.png
evals.expected_output: Severity-bucketed findings covering at least error states (what happens if payment fails), edge cases (zero items, maximum items), and accessibility (contrast, tap targets). Top-fixes list with at least 3 entries. Step 1/5 through Step 5/5 headers emitted. Each finding states broken state and user consequence in one sentence.
evals.actual_output: Emitted Step 1/5 through Step 5/5. Flagged screen mismatch (three.png is a browse screen, not checkout). Blocker B1: screen does not match PRD — no checkout path exists. Contrast finding included. Top Fixes: 5 items including resolve routing error as #1.

assertions.text: Output contains 'Step 1/5' through 'Step 5/5' headers in order
Output includes at least one finding from the error-state taxonomy (loading, error, offline, empty, permission)
Output includes at least one WCAG or accessibility finding (contrast, tap target, or screen reader)
Output includes a Top Fixes section with at least 3 entries
Output flags the mismatch between the PRD (checkout screen) and the actual image (browse screen)

evals.prompt:
Look at this mockup and tell me what's wrong with it from a UX perspective. Here's the product brief: We are building a food ordering app. This screen is the home feed. The primary CTA is to browse and order food. Users are on mobile (iOS and Android). We want users to complete their first order in under 3 minutes. We have no offline state currently designed.

Screenshot URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/afgprogrammer/Flutter-food-delivery-app-ui/master/assets/screenshot/one.png
evals.expected_output: Scope check passes (in-scope UX request). Review identifies at least one gap from error-state taxonomy (specifically: offline/loading/empty states missing). At least one WCAG finding (contrast or tap target). Top fixes prioritise by user impact. Step 1/5 through Step 5/5 headers present. Refusal templates NOT invoked since request is in-scope.
evals.actual_output: Emitted Step 1/5 through Step 5/5. Scope: in-scope, no refusal invoked. Error-state taxonomy gaps named: Empty, Loading, Error, Offline, Permission. WCAG findings: contrast failure SC 1.4.3; non-descriptive VoiceOver label SC 4.1.2. Blocker: 4 findings. Major: 7 findings. Top Fixes: 5 items ranked by user impact.

assertions.text: Output contains 'Step 1/5' through 'Step 5/5' headers in order
Output does not invoke a refusal template — scope_decision is in-scope
Output names at least one of: offline state, loading state, or empty state as a missing error state
Output includes at least one WCAG finding naming contrast (SC 1.4.3) or tap target size
Top Fixes section is present and items are described as ranked by user impact

_examples.good_assertion: Output includes a severity-grouped section headed Blocker with at least one finding
_examples.verdict_rule: verdict = pass only if every assertion.passed === true; otherwise fail
